fullText: for joining us for write cleaner safer modern c plus plus code with sonar cube before we get to the content i need to hit some housekeeping notes there we go that went too far so first this will be a 30 minute session including some brief demos and q a we are recording the presentation during the presentation you can use the q a box at any time to ask questions we do have some panelists waiting in the wings to help you out with that stuff in a few days from today you'll receive an email with a link to the recording of this session as well as all the questions asked during q a whether or not they got answered today the follow-up will include answers to all the questions at the end you'll receive a feedback survey please please please do fill it out tell us how we did tell us what we need to work on tell us what we did well tell us what you'd like to see us cover in future webinars a brief agenda i'll start with some introductions uh we'll talk about what we can find in your code you'll see some product in action demos to show where we can find stuff in your code we'll talk about integrating the tools into your systems and then how to get them so here we go again thanks for coming to write cleaner safer modern c plus plus code with sonar cube my name is anne campbell i'm a coder and a sonar sourcer since 2013. i've been active in the community since long before that so if you've ever interacted in the sonar source community then we've probably encountered each other so hello again um i'm joined today by look julie lolick would you mind saying hello hello everybody so loic is a c-plus plus expert and a sonar sorcerer since 2018. his day job is crafting c plus rules to find interesting things in your code and in his spare time he represents france on the iso committee for c plus plus as well as being a member of the misra c plus plus committee and they're currently working on the next version of that standard i also want to introduce the company sonar source was founded in 2008 by three developers who came together with the goal of writing tools to help other developers improve their code quality and security specifically they wanted to write tools for developers and development teams not managers but developers that are simple and transparent easy to integrate into your workflow and accurate and helpful always and specifically what that means is no false positives we're human it happens but when you find a false positive please do tell us we will be all over it and get it fixed as quickly as possible now those three guys that i mentioned started with java because they were java developers and we have huge adoption in the java community we're popular with thousands of developers of java and a number of other languages we've since expanded to cover 27 languages including c plus plus and we consider c plus plus one of our flagships and what that means is that in c plus plus you're going to get just as rich and valuable and
